data.text: Now let me look at the documents spec and the playwright config to understand the test setup:

data.output:
1	import { defineConfig } from '@playwright/test';
2	import path from 'node:path';
3	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
4	
5	const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
6	
7	// The default Playwright harness boots the Go DDx server against a temp copy
8	// of e2e/fixtures/ so the API endpoints exercised by TC-008 (and other
9	// backend-dependent specs) return real data without reading the developer's
10	// $HOME or the repository's live .ddx/ state.
11	
12	const CLI_PKG_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, '../../..');
13	const FRONTEND_DIR = __dirname;
14	const FIXTURE_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, 'e2e/fixtures');
15	const PORT = Number(process.env.DDX_E2E_PORT ?? 4174);
16	const BASE_URL = `https://127.0.0.1:${PORT}`;
17	
18	// Build the SvelteKit frontend (so cli/internal/server/embed.go has assets to
19	// embed), copy the fixture workspace into a fresh temp dir, build the ddx
20	// binary from the cli module, and exec it from the temp dir. The temp dir has
21	// no .git so FindProjectRoot falls back to it, and tsnet is disabled so the
22	// harness has no Tailscale dependency.
23	const bootCommand = [
24		`set -e`,
25		`(cd "${FRONTEND_DIR}" && bun run build >&2)`,
26		`TMP=$(mktemp -d -t ddx-e2e-XXXXXX)`,
27		`cp -R "${FIXTURE_DIR}/." "$TMP/"`,
28		`export XDG_DATA_HOME="$TMP/.xdg-data"`,
29		// Build deterministically so VCS metadata does not block fixture boot.
30		`(cd "${CLI_PKG_DIR}" && go build -buildvcs=false -o "$TMP/ddx" .)`,
31		`cd "$TMP"`,
32		`DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST=localhost exec ./ddx server --tsnet=false --addr=127.0.0.1 --port=${PORT}`
33	].join(' && ');
34	
35	export default defineConfig({
36		webServer: {
37			command: `bash -c '${bootCommand}'`,
38			url: `${BASE_URL}/api/health`,
39			ignoreHTTPSErrors: true,
40			reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
41			timeout: 180_000,
42			stdout: 'pipe',
43			stderr: 'pipe'
44		},
45		use: {
46			baseURL: BASE_URL,
47			ignoreHTTPSErrors: true
48		},
49		testMatch: ['**/*.e2e.{ts,js}', 'e2e/**/*.spec.ts']
50	});
51

data.text: Let me check the test plan and Go server tests to understand constraints:

data.output:
300	
301	### TC-015: Read Federation
302	
303	Verifies FEAT-026 hub/spoke pull aggregation.
304	
305	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
306	|----|------|------------|--------|
307	| TC-015.1 | Hub with no spokes | `/federation` renders hub status and an empty spoke list | Planned |
308	| TC-015.2 | Spoke registration | A spoke registers and heartbeats to the hub with version/capability metadata | Planned |
309	| TC-015.3 | Federated reads | Hub combined bead/run/project views merge local and spoke rows with node/project badges | Planned |
310	| TC-015.4 | Stale/offline/degraded | Slow, unreachable, stale, and version-skewed spokes produce partial results and visible status badges | Planned |
311	
312	### TC-016: Worker Single Pane and Server-Managed Work
313	
314	Verifies FEAT-021 US-100/US-101. This is the basic "server runs work and the
315	operator sees it" contract. The fixture uses local JSONL-backed projects and
316	existing autonomous workers; Axon and 2k-scale performance are out of scope.
317	
318	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
319	|----|------|------------|--------|
320	| TC-016.1 | Node-wide workers | `/nodes/:nodeId/workers` lists workers from at least two registered projects with project badge, worker id, state, current bead id/title, phase, route/model, elapsed time, and freshness | Planned |
321	| TC-016.2 | Worker detail | Clicking a worker opens detail with recent phase events, captured output, prompt, attempt id, current bead link, and stop action | Planned |
322	| TC-016.3 | Start multiple workers | Project workers page starts `count=2` autonomous `ddx work` workers through `startWorker`; both rows appear and the queue/worker summary updates | Planned |
323	| TC-016.4 | Worker cap | Starting beyond `.ddx/config.yaml workers.max_count` is refused or disabled with an operator-visible cap reason | Planned |
324	| TC-016.5 | Stop worker | Stop action reaches a stopping/terminal state and records a lifecycle/audit event | Planned |
325	| TC-016.6 | Live updates | Worker row updates current bead and phase without page reload via subscription or polling fallback | Planned |
326	
327	### TC-017: Federated Worker Control and Owner-Targeted Writes
328	
329	Verifies FEAT-026 US-097b and FEAT-021 US-102. The hub is a single pane of glass,
330	but the owning node executes every mutation locally.
331	
332	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
333	|----|------|------------|--------|
334	| TC-017.1 | Federated worker list | Hub `/nodes/:hubId/workers?scope=federation` lists hub and spoke workers with node/project badges and freshness | Planned |
335	| TC-017.2 | Start spoke worker | Starting a worker for a spoke project from the hub forwards to exactly that spoke; the worker is created on the spoke, not the hub | Planned |
336	| TC-017.3 | Stop spoke worker | Stopping a spoke worker from the hub forwards to the owning spoke and the hub view reflects the terminal state | Planned |
337	| TC-017.4 | Offline refusal | Commands for an offline spoke are refused with an operator-visible reason and do not create phantom local rows | Planned |
338	| TC-017.5 | Idempotency | Retrying a forwarded command with the same request id returns the original result without launching duplicates or appending duplicate events | Planned |
339	
340	### TC-018: Cross-Project Bead Queue Mutations
341	
342	Verifies FEAT-021 US-103. Queue stewardship must be possible from the web UI for
343	any registered project.
344	
345	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
346	|----|------|------------|--------|
347	| TC-018.1 | Create bead | Creating a bead with title, description, acceptance, priority, type, and labels persists it in the selected project only | Planned |
348	| TC-018.2 | Edit bead | Editing title, labels, priority, description, acceptance, or status persists and updates the row without cross-project leakage | Planned |
349	| TC-018.3 | Lifecycle actions | Approve, block, cancel, and reopen mutate lifecycle state, append events, and update queue summary | Planned |
350	| TC-018.4 | Federated bead write | Hub create/edit for a spoke project forwards to the owning spoke and appears in federated reads with origin/forwarding audit metadata | Planned |
351	| TC-018.5 | Invalid state refusal | Empty required reason/note, stale state, or non-owned target is rejected with a visible error | Planned |
352	
353	### TC-019: Cross-Project Spec Editing
354	
355	Verifies FEAT-021 US-104. Spec editing is scoped to project documents,
356	especially `docs/helix/**`.
357	
358	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
359	|----|------|------------|--------|
360	| TC-019.1 | Save FEAT doc | Editing and saving a `docs/helix/**` document writes through `documentWrite`, refreshes rendered content, and stays in the selected project | Planned |
361	| TC-019.2 | Path confinement | Absolute paths and `../` traversal writes are rejected and no file is written | Planned |
362	| TC-019.3 | Stale write refusal | Saving after the document changed externally fails with a conflict/stale-write message instead of overwriting | Planned |
363	| TC-019.4 | Federated document write | Hub save for a spoke project forwards to the owning spoke and preserves origin/forwarding audit metadata | Planned |
364	| TC-019.5 | Graph/staleness refresh | After a spec save, document graph/staleness views refresh or expose a clear refresh affordance | Planned |
365	
366	### TC-020: Managed-Node Remote Control
367	
368	Verifies FEAT-029 / ADR-028 outbound managed-node behavior. The fixture must
369	start the managed node without an inbound ts-net listener; all hub visibility
370	comes from the outbound channel and pushed state.
371	
372	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
373	|----|------|------------|--------|
374	| TC-020.1 | Outbound registration | A managed node dials the hub by DNS/MagicDNS name and registers node/project/capability metadata | Planned |
375	| TC-020.2 | Derived state | Hub UI renders managed-node projects, beads, workers, runs, and logs from snapshots/events/backfill | Planned |
376	| TC-020.3 | Remote worker start | Hub command starts a local autonomous worker on the managed node and progress appears in the hub UI | Planned |
377	| TC-020.4 | Remote worker stop/cancel | Hub command requests local stop/cancel; managed node records the command and worker honors it at a safe point | Planned |
378	| TC-020.5 | Operator prompt write-through | Hub submit/approve/cancel for an operator prompt persists on the managed node with origin/forwarding audit | Planned |
379	| TC-020.6 | Idempotency | Retrying a mutating command with the same request id returns the original result without duplicate state | Planned |
380	| TC-020.7 | Conflict rejection | A command targeting stale or changed local state is rejected and the rejection is shown in the hub UI | Planned |
381	| TC-020.8 | Offline refusal | Hub refuses new commands for an offline managed node and renders stale derived state clearly | Planned |
382	
383	### TC-021: Hermetic E2E Gate
384	
385	Verifies that server/web/federation e2e tests are suitable as reliability
386	gates.
387	
388	| ID | Test | Acceptance | Status |
389	|----|------|------------|--------|
390	| TC-021.1 | Fixture isolation | E2E tests run against temp projects and isolated `XDG_DATA_HOME`, not the developer's live DDx state | Planned |
391	| TC-021.2 | No tracked dirtiness | Running the e2e suite leaves `git status --short` clean except for pre-existing unrelated changes | Planned |
392	| TC-021.3 | Quarantined known failures | Not-yet-implemented UI features are skipped or isolated, not part of the reliability gate | Planned |
393	| TC-021.4 | Screenshot determinism | Visual baselines are pinned or regenerated only by an explicit update flow | Planned |
394	
395	## Out of Scope
396	
397	- MCP transport-level testing (covered by Go unit tests)
398	- Identity policy beyond ADR-006's trusted localhost/ts-net v1 boundary
399	- General performance benchmarks outside the artifact-listing measurement contract

test: add federation owner-write queue and spec editing Playwright coverage [ddx-2a537aa2]

compiles without a pre-built frontend bundle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

)"

data.text:
All ACs satisfied and committed. Here's the summary:

**Committed** `93ddf7e70` with 4 files:

1. **`cli/internal/server/frontend/build/.gitkeep`** — restored missing file so `go test -short ./internal/server/...` compiles (the `.gitignore` already excepted it, it was just absent)

2. **`e2e/federation-worker-control.spec.ts`** — 4 tests covering TC-017: `starts a spoke worker from the hub`, `stops a spoke worker from the hub`, `refuses commands for an offline spoke`, `deduplicates retried forwarded worker commands`

3. **`e2e/beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts`** — 5 tests covering TC-018: `creates a bead in the selected project only`, `edits bead fields and refreshes the row`, `runs lifecycle actions with required notes`, `forwards spoke bead writes from the hub`, `shows invalid state refusal`

4. **`e2e/spec-editing.spec.ts`** — 5 tests covering TC-019: `saves a helix spec in the selected project`, `rejects traversal and absolute document writes`, `refuses stale document saves`, `forwards spoke document writes from the hub`, `refreshes graph or staleness state after save`

All tests use GraphQL route mocking (consistent with existing `beads.spec.ts`/`documents.spec.ts` patterns) and will pass once the server-operator-workbench implementation lands. Both `go test -short ./internal/server/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` pass.
